mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals an ongoing monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 4% indicates a tight labor market that drives wage inflation and corporate cost pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE < 4%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns falls below -0.2 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in residential construction (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) signals strong consumer demand that drives corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between HOUST and RSAFS shows a coefficient < 0.1 in 3-year rolling regressions, or SPX returns in high-demand regimes are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 3-month moving average indicates weakening supply chain capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO < 3mo MA, or the correlation between INDPRO and SPX returns exceeds -0.15 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate (GDPC1) above 3% signals robust economic expansion that boosts corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in high-GDP regimes are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the GDP-SPX return correlation falls below 0.2 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 12-month decline in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) below 2% indicates disinflationary pressures that prompt central bank rate cuts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL < 2%, or the CPI-SPX return correlation exceeds 0.15 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates labor market mismatch that raises corporate labor costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE shows a coefficient < 0.05 in 3-year rolling regressions, or SPX returns in mismatched labor regimes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 12-month high signals strong supply-side momentum that drives corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO > 12mo high, or the INDPRO-SPX return correlation falls below 0.2 in rolling 1-year windows.
